title: Heliosperma villosum
taxonomy: Heliosperma villosum is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Heliosperma, which is a group of flowering plants. The species name 'villosum' refers to the hairy or villous appearance of the plant. Heliosperma villosum is native to the Mediterranean region and is found in areas with rocky terrain. It is a perennial plant, meaning it grows and blooms over multiple seasons.

intro: Heliosperma villosum, also known as Ajuga chamaepitys, is a perennial plant species within the mint family, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Heliosperma villosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
